join_order.notes: Populate a dense customer-membership bitset from customer_mktsegment_postings, then test customer_bitset[o_custkey] during a sequential orders scan after zone-map pruning on o_orderdate.

join_order.build: orders(filtered by customer and orderdate, in o_orderkey order)
join_order.notes: Use the qualifying order row id to read lineitem_orderkey_groups row_starts/row_counts directly and aggregate each order's contiguous lineitem run immediately, without materializing large qualifying-order payload arrays.

data_structures.fact_join: direct_array(customer membership on o_custkey during sequential orders scan; row-aligned lookup into lineitem_orderkey_groups)
data_structures.aggregation: array_direct(per-order scalar revenue accumulation with thread-local topk heaps)

indexes.purpose: build dense customer-membership bitset for BUILDING
skip and fast-accept order blocks during sequential orders scan
direct row-aligned access to contiguous lineitem runs per qualifying order

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current plan spends almost all time in build_joins because it drives orders through orders_custkey_postings from qualifying customers, creating many random probes into orders metadata and materializing large intermediate qualifying-order arrays before scanning lineitem. Strategy category A (plan restructuring): build a tiny dense customer-membership bitset from the customer postings, then scan orders sequentially with orders_orderdate_zone_map pruning, test customer membership inline, and immediately probe the row-aligned lineitem_orderkey_groups to aggregate revenue per qualifying order in o_orderkey order. This replaces random index-nested-loop behavior with cache-friendly sequential access and fuses join plus aggregation to eliminate the expensive intermediate buffers.
